tldr: Headlines are overwhelmingly dominated by Israeli military strikes continuing despite a notional ceasefire — fresh air attacks, civilian casualties, and drone strikes — with Washington ceasefire talks treated as a thin diplomatic veneer; morality-register headlines about religious-site desecration and a displacement story round out a corpus that shows little daylight between ongoing war and nominal peace.

evidence.quote: Israel launches fresh strikes on Lebanon despite ceasefire
Israel steps up 'brutal bombardment' in southern Lebanon
Israel strikes cars on Lebanese highway, killing 8, including 2 children
Lebanon says two paramedics among 13 killed in Israeli strikes
Lebanon, Israel begin talks on 'disputed issues' in Washington
Lebanon, Israel meet in the US for new ceasefire talks
Churches under attack in southern Lebanon
Israeli soldiers jailed for desecrating Virgin Mary statue in Lebanon
Hezbollah support endures in south Lebanon as ceasefire fails
the Christian border town in Lebanon that says it survived by defying Hezbollah
Sport helping displaced Lebanese living in stadium to cope

isolation_top.note: Vatican News leads with 'Churches under attack' — exclusively religious-sites framing with no military or diplomatic vocabulary shared with the corpus
IRNA headline foregrounds Hezbollah chief's 'resistance' and 'not surrender' language — resistance-ideology register distinct from factual-strike corpus
Fox News headlines the Christian border town's defiance of Hezbollah — civilian-agency and sectarian framing absent from other titles
Deutsche Welle leads with sport-as-coping in a displaced-persons stadium — quality-of-life register semantically isolated from air-strike corpus

silences.what_they_covered_instead: France leads the UNIFIL peacekeeping force in southern Lebanon and would have the most direct operational view of the buffer zone; absent from corpus
Italy has one of the largest UNIFIL contingents in southern Lebanon; its perspective on buffer-zone enforcement is unrepresented
Lebanese government officials are mentioned in headlines through pan_arab and south_africa, but no Lebanon-sourced outlet appears in corpus — the occupied party's direct voice is absent

single_outlet_findings.finding: Only outlet leading with an attack on Christian churches specifically — the only headline that treats religious-site destruction as the primary news angle
Only outlet leading with a criminal-accountability headline about soldiers jailed for desecrating a Virgin Mary statue — frames Israeli conduct through CRIME_PUNISHMENT lens absent elsewhere
Only outlet framing a Lebanese civilian community as anti-Hezbollah actors who survived by resistance — inverts the dominant victimhood framing of other titles
Only outlet specifying Israeli attacks north of the Litani River to relieve pressure on troops at a 'crucial axis' — operational-geography framing absent from all other headlines

bottom_line: Headline coverage of Lebanon's buffer zone reads as an ongoing war barely dressed in ceasefire language — strikes on civilians, paramedics, and churches dominate, while Washington talks appear as a secondary diplomatic ritual rather than an active conflict-resolution mechanism.
